content.full_text:
Memorandum

To: Nancy Conzett

Date: December 29, 1977

From: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        Dwight Dickerson

Subject: Phone Advice

Our office received a letter from Menlo Park Council Member Jennifer Bigelow dated December 8, 1977. In that letter she asked us to consider the following: In the near future, a matter pertaining to the development of a parcel of land adjacent to a piece of property upon which she resides will be considered by the Council. The question she raises is whether she is disqualified from participating in the matter pertaining to the development.

On December 22, 1977, I advised her by phone that Government Code §§87100 and 87103(b) stated that she had to disqualify herself from participating in a governmental decision where it is foreseeable that the decision could have a material effect on her property interest. I pointed out to her that the Commission offered guidelines in Regulation 18702(b)(2) for determining when a material financial effect would be present.

I made it clear to Council Member Bigelow that the guidelines offered by the Commission were not conclusive. The Commission stated in 18702(a) that if the official knows or has reason to know that their financial interest could impair their ability to be free from bias, materiality would be present, and the official would probably have to be disqualified if the other elements were present.

DD:ma

December 8, 1977

State of California  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, Calif. 95804

Gentlemen:

I am a member of the Menlo Park City Council.

In the near future, a matter will be brought before the City Council pertaining to the development of a parcel of land adjacent to a piece of property where I reside.

I have no financial or real interests in the parcel subject to development.

As a member of the Council, should I disqualify myself from participating in making a decision affecting this property?

I am requesting your ruling in order to avoid the possibility of a "conflict of interest" controversy arising.

I look forward to hearing from you at your earliest convenience.

Sincerely,

Jennifer Bigelow  
Council Member
